about.title: L'Art Brut 9, Madge Gill, Bentivegna, Ratier, divers autres et la collection du Dr Marie.
about.referencement: DUMARÇAY, Philippe. First edition by the Compagnie de l'Art Brut.
about.description: I bought this book at the Collection d'Art Brut in Lausanne to document the gymnasium thesis I was writing. I was interested by Madge Gill's work who ended up being one of the topics in my essay.

about.title: L'Art Brut 2, Adolf Wölfli.
about.referencement: MORGENTHALER, Walter. First edition by the Compagnie de l'Art Brut.
about.description: I bought this book at the Collection d'Art Brut in Lausanne to document the gymnasium thesis I was writing. I ended up choosing Adolf Wölfli as one of my subjects.

about.title: Artificial nature.
about.referencement: DEITCH, Jeffrey, FRIEDMAN, Dan. Published by Deste Foundation for Contemporary Arts.
about.description: I found this book at an Emaüs store in Sion for 2 CHF and had no idea what it was back then. Turns out it was an important inspiration for my future in the graphic world.

about.title: Ce que l'on s'aime.
about.referencement: BOUTAGHANE, Olympe, CUPSA, ALexandra, VANDEWALLE, Chloé. Published by Éditions V1.
about.description: This book is very special to me as it was published by my friends. I contributed to some of the texts inside.

about.title: The Convent of Pleasure.
about.referencement: CAVENDISH, Margaret. Edited by RENTIEN LANDO Sophie, published by Espace Ness.
about.description: I bought this book at the P.A.G.E.S. book festival in Geneva. It's a feminist theatrical piece by Margaret Cavendish.

about.title: Mockup x36
about.referencement: FANTYS, Pierre, RAPPO, François. Published by ECAL, école cantonale d'art de Lausanne.
about.description: This book was acquired during an archive book sale at écal. It was 2 CHF/kg!

about.title: Pour une critique du design graphique.
about.referencement: DE SMET, Catherine. Published by Éditions B42.
about.description: I bought this book at La Fnac to explore more theoretical aspects of graphic design.

about.title: Keystone.
about.referencement: Published by Sunset.
about.description: This book was given to me by my ex’s mother, who is a photographer. It's an early 00s catalogue of stock photos you could purchase for your projects.

about.title: KIP
about.description: This is a collaborative photography zine by (among others) a photographer called Daniela K. Monteiro. I found her work on Instagram.

about.title: Lucy, by Lucy Garland - volume 1.
about.referencement: GARLAND, Lucy. Self published.
about.description: This coffee table book is an incredible self-published work by the multidisciplinary artist Lucy Garland.

about.title: Émile Gallé.
about.referencement: GALLÉ, Émile. Published by Parkstone Press International.
about.description: I found this book by chance outside a bookstore in Sion (where I was studying back then). It was in one of those "on sale" boxes, and I couldn't resist picking it up.

about.title: Bon à tirer, guide d'optimisation d'impression.
about.referencement: ECAL, Ecole cantonale d’art de Lausanne.
about.description: Parts of this book were made during a workshop with écal's photolithographer James Pasal. An edition was then given to everyone in the class.

about.title: Y2K20
about.referencement: ECAL, école cantonale d'art de lausanne.
about.description: The Y2K20 book is special to me as I contributed to its production during a workshop with a professor of mine, Guy Meldem.

about.title: Mots.
about.referencement: SCARRY, Richard. Published by Editions Chantecler.
about.description: I fell in love with this tiny book (it must not be over 9x9cm) the moment I saw it in this antique shop I adore in St-Maurice. Even if it is for children, it is one of my favorite books in my collection.

about.title: Service ac pour soldats, manuel d'instruction audio-visuelle.
about.referencement: Edited by EM Gemg, DIV Spac
about.description: I found this book in an antique shop called "Le Bois Noir." It was an instruction manual given to Swiss army soldiers in 1975.

about.title: Monsters: What Do We Do With Great Art By Bad People?
about.referencement: DEDERER, Claire.
about.description: I bought this book at the library of the Tate Museum in London.

about.title: Rasmus Klump, Har Flyvegriller.
about.referencement: Published by Pixi Carlsen.
about.description: A tiny book I bought at the same time as a crêpe in the amusement park "Tivoli" in Copenhagen.

about.title: Grilles.
about.referencement: AMBROSE, Gavin, HARRIS, Paul. Published by Pyramyd Éditions.
about.description: This book was in my father's archive. He gave it to me when I entered écal.

about.title: Comprendre la typographie.
about.referencement: LUPTON, Ellen. Published by Pyramyd Éditions.
about.description: This book was in my father's archive. He gave it to me when I entered écal.

about.title: Maquette.
about.referencement: AMBROSE, Gavin, HARRIS, Paul. Published by Pyramyd Éditions.
about.description: This book was in my father's archive. He gave it to me when I entered écal.

about.title: Hypothetical Death of the Exarchian Alpha.
about.referencement: VILLAUMIÉ, Ella. Published by DOLCE publishing and sun/sun éditions.
about.description: I bought this book at the P.A.G.E.S. book festival in Geneva.

about.title: ECAL: Design Graphique/Grophic Design Design/Grafik Design.
about.referencement: BOVIER, Lionel. Published by ECAL, Ecole cantonale d’art de Lausanne.
about.description: This book was acquired during an archive book sale at écal. It was only 2 CHF/kg!

about.title: The most beatiful Swiss books - 2003.
about.referencement: Published by Federal Office of Culture.
about.description: This book was given to me at Christmas by the écal librarian as a "reward" for using the library a lot!

about.title: Eames & Vitra.
about.referencement: Published by Vitra.
about.description: This book was a Christmas gift from my big sister, who was then working in a design furniture agency. She received it from there and then gave it to me!

about.title: Grid system in graphic design.
about.referencement: MÜLLER-BROCKMANN, Joseph. 19th edition published by Niggli.
about.description: I bought this book as a joke because "You should learn it, and then burn it."

about.title: Brigade Cynophile, posters 2019-2023.
about.referencement: LANDRIVON, Félicité. First edition published by Animal Press.
about.description: I bought this book at the P.A.G.E.S. book festival in Geneva. It was very special to meet Félicité, whose work I love.

about.title: Glossary of Undisciplined Design.
about.referencement: KAISER, Anja. Published by Spector Books.
about.description: This book was given to me by Anja Kaiser. We had a workshop with her and she brought a lot of books, including this one in multiple copies. When I asked if I could buy one, she told me I could just take one!

about.title: What Design Can’t Do, Essays on Design and Disillusion.
about.referencement: LORUSSO, Silvio.  Published by Set Margins'.
about.description: I found out about this book on Instagram and bought it on the Set Margins website.

about.title: Design for All? Inclusive Design Today.
about.referencement: STEINER, Evelyn and ZELLER Sara.  Edited by Museum für Gestaltung Zürich, published by Spector Books.
about.description: I found out about this book via a friend of mine who was doing her bachelor thesis at the time. When I saw it at the P.A.G.E.S. book festival in Geneva, I bought it.

about.title: Printmaking by ECAL 2008-2014.
about.referencement: SCHMIDLIN Laurence, LYON Anne-Catherine, ENCKELL JULLIARD Julie, GEORGACOPOULOS Alexis, SERRA Stéphanie. Published by Musée Jenisch Vevey, Cabinet cantonal des estampes and ECAL, école cantonale d'art de Lausanne.
about.description: This is an archival book about printmaking at écal. I bought it during the archive sale at écal.

about.title: Words Form Language.
about.referencement: MAGER, Simon. Published by Triest.
about.description: This book was given to me as a gift for responding to a survey concerning the HES-SO.

about.title: Maximage Color Combinations.
about.referencement: Maximage and ECAL.  Published by ECAL, école cantonale d'art de Lausanne.
about.description: This book was acquired during an archive book sale at écal. It is super useful when it comes to laser printing.

about.title: ECAL Graphic Design.
about.referencement: ECAL.  Published by JRP|Ringier.
about.description: This book was given to me by a friend who won a bingo organized by écal in Zurich. He already had the book, so he gave it to me.

about.title: Symbolic Machines – Institutional Transformation Through Exhibitions.
about.referencement: Edited my Pernille Lystlund Matzen & Jacob Lund.
about.description: I bought this book at the Louisiana Museum in Copenhagen. It was my holiday reading material.

about.title: The Most Beautiful Swiss Books 2023: An Autopsy of Tastes and Values.
about.referencement: BORN, Julia, ROTHENBERGER Sereina, WÄLCHLI Tan.  Published by Federal Office of Culture.
about.description: I received this catalogue as a gift from Nicole Udry, who gave one to every graduating student. (I wasn't a graduating student but took one anyway.)

about.title: Swiss Design Awards 2020. Failure is not an Option?
about.referencement: Published by Federal Office of Culture.
about.description: I found this book for free in the "to give away" area of écal's library. I later had a professor who had designed this book. Sometimes I find it funny how everything is linked.

about.title: On Words, volume 4.
about.referencement: BURKHALTER, Sarah, ENCKELL, Julie, MARTINI Federica.Published by Scheidegger & Spiess, SIK-ISEA.
about.description: It was my second Christmas library gift and is a book made by one of my teachers. He had shown it to us earlier in the semester. I was really happy to have it, as I liked this teacher's methods a lot.

about.title: Un féminisme décolonial.
about.referencement: VERGÈS, Françoise.  Published by La Fabrique éditions.
about.description: I bought this book after having a discussion with colleagues as part of a lecture I took with Joël Vacheron called “Global Vision.”

about.title: À propos d'amour.
about.referencement: hooks, bell. Published by Divergences.
about.description: I bought this book after I finished "The Will to Change: Men, Masculinity,and Love" by the same author.

about.title: Cahier de recherche – Diversions 1.
about.referencement: KOIVU, Anniina.  Published by ECAL, école cantonale d'art de Lausanne.
about.description: At écal's library, there's a nook where you can find free books to take home. I found this one there.

about.title: Le Dico Romand.
about.referencement: SUTER, Henry.Published by ECAL/Ecole cantonale d’art de Lausanne, Edition Favre.
about.description: ECAL, Ecole cantonale d’art de Lausanne. Le Dico romand, lexique de chez nous. Published by Editions Favre. Lausanne, 2021. This book was acquired during an archive book sale at écal. I bought two—one for me and one for my mum.

about.title: Vacances Tranquilou, en France avec Aldo
about.referencement: GALIPIENSO, Maxime. Published by Requins Marteaux.

about.title: Who can afford to be critical? An inquiry into what we can't do alors, as designers, and into what we might be able to do together, as people.
about.referencement: MATOS, Afonso. Published by Set Margins'.
about.description: I found this book on Instagram and got curious about its content. I was happy to discover it was a collaborative work.

about.title: Automated photography.
about.referencement: KELLER, Milo, GUNTI, Claus, AMOSER, Florian  Published by Mörel Books and ECAL, école cantonale d’art de Lausanne.
about.description: During an archive book sale at écal, everyone would receive a copy of this book in its bag. It had a manufacturing defect and couldn't be sold. Instead of throwing them away,they gave them to us!

about.title: Bettina Rheims - 40th Ed.
about.referencement: REMY, Patrick. Published by Tashen.
about.description: I bought this book in Vevey during "Images Vevey", a photography festival that takes place throughout the entire town.

about.title: L'idiot utile - Issue 0.
about.referencement: JULIER, Raphaël and ORTEGA, Leyre  Published by ces éditions and LeMégot éditions.
about.description: I bought this book at the store of the Swiss Design Award in Basel. We went with my class and got to swim in the Rhine afterwards.

about.title: 1020 Renens.
about.referencement: Published by ECAL, Ville de Renens.
about.description: My father gifted me this book during the écal Christmas market.

about.title: Jean Otth, Travaux/Works 1964-2013.
about.referencement: SCHWEIZER, Nicole.  Published by Musée cantonal des beaux-Arts and Scheidegger & Spiess.
about.description: I bought this book at the MCBA in Lausanne before living here. We went with my art class to a Jean Otth exhibition, who I did not know back then. I loved his work. When I started écal, I found out he used to be a professor there. Later, one of my professors, Nicole Udry, told me that he used to be her teacher.

about.title: Second Nature.
about.referencement: WISE, Chloe. Published by Almine Rech Editions.
about.description: I don't remember where I bought this book (probably on the internet), but I remember it was a time when I was obsessed with Chloe Wise's work — especially the backgrounds of her portrait paintings.

about.title: The Sniper In The Brain.
about.referencement: PAGIN, Jacopo.  First edition by NERO and MAKE ROOM.
about.description: I love the work of the artist Jacopo Pagin. My partner gifted me his book for my birthday. Fun fact: I had told everyone I wanted this book. Turns out my dad also bought it for my birthday.

about.title: Épopées célestes – Art brut dans la collection Decharme.
about.referencement: GIACOSA, Gustavo and SAFAROVA, Barbara. Published by Éditions Empire.
about.description: I've always loved Art Brut ever since I discovered it. This book was not available in Switzerland. I wanted it so badly that I asked a colleague of mine who lives in France to have it sent to his house so he could give it to me later. The cover of this book was the biggest inspiration for my project "The Greatest Show on Earth!(?), Freak Shows Through Headlines".

about.title: HELLO My name is.
about.referencement: LEE-COHEN, Nadia.  Published by IDEA.
about.description: I love the work and world of Nadia Lee Cohen. I got the book as a birthday gift from my sister.

about.title: Andy Warhol, Polaroids 1958–1987.
about.referencement: GOLDEN, Reuel.  Published by Taschen.
about.description: I bought this book at the store of the Kunsthaus in Zürich. Fun fact: I was supposed to go with a friend of mine who stood me up. So I spent the day alone in Zürich — and it was actually quite nice.

about.title: 032c — Issue 39.
about.referencement: Published by 032c.
about.description: This is the only issue of 032c I have. It features a broadsheet fashion zine that I find fantastic.

about.title: Seen Through Others.
about.referencement: CHENG, Xinyi. Published by Lafayette Anticipations.
about.description: I bought this book at the MCBA in Lausanne. What's funny is that I bought it because I liked the object. I later found the artist by chance on Instagram. I then visited — also by chance — Lafayette Anticipations Gallery, the publisher.

about.title: La main (et) le gant.
about.description: I go every year to Images Vevey, and this year, I decided to explore the "Musée Jenisch", where I found this book that is very interesting, form-wise.

about.title: Contemporary Canadian Art.
about.referencement: BURNETT, David and SCHIFF, Marilyn.  Published by Hurig and The Art Gallery of Ontario.
about.description: I found this book at an Emaüs store in Sion and took it without looking at the content too closely. Turns out my great-uncle is featured in it. His name was Rodolphe de Repentigny, known as Jauran.

about.title: Del Llanto.
about.referencement: BANBAN, Cristina.  Published by 1969 Gallery and Albertz Benda.
about.description: This is a book by the incredible artist Cristina BanBan. Her vision of the feminine body influenced me a lot in my drawing as well as painting.

about.title: Collection d'art de la Banque Cantonale Vaudoise.
about.referencement: SCHORRO, Michal Florence, LADOUX, Simon, SIMON-VERMOT, Prune, TAUER, Katharina.
about.description: I found this book for free in the "to give away" area of écal's library.

about.title: Raw Vision Magazine, issue 18.
about.description: This is an Art Brut magazine I bought when I was writing my maturity paper. I was looking for specific pictures of Adolf Wölfli's work but couldn't find good quality ones, so I subscribed to this magazine — and I’m still subscribed to their newsletter (that I never read).

about.title: Novembre Magazine, Issue 12.
about.description: I bought this issue at écal's archive book sale. I got lucky because there weren’t many — only one or two boxes — and I happened to be standing next to the janitor who was opening the last one.

about.title: TYPEONE Magazine — Issue 09, Packaging, Food and Typography.
about.referencement: Published by TYPEONE Magazine.
about.description: I have a subscription to "Typeone magazine". There used to be two issues per year, but now there's only one. That gives me time to forget about it, be confused when I receive it, and be happy when I open it and remember.

about.title: TYPEONE Magazine — Issue 06, The Experimental Type Issue.
about.referencement: Published by TYPEONE Magazine.
about.description: I have a subscription to "Typeone magazine" and this is the sixth issue — my first one.

about.title: TYPEONE Magazine — Issue 08, The Japanese Graphic Design x Typography Issue.
about.referencement: Published by TYPEONE Magazine.
about.description: I have a subscription to "Typeone magazine" and this is the eighth issue. For a journaling class, I had to interview someone and chose Amber Weaver, the director of the magazine. She never answered my emails :-/

about.title: TYPEONE Magazine — Issue 07, The Creative Coding Issue
about.referencement: Published by TYPEONE Magazine.
about.description: I have a subscription to "Typeone magazine" and this is the seventh issue.

about.title: LIGURIA: Recipes & Wanderings
about.referencement: Published by Apartamento Publishing S.L.
about.description: This book was a gift from my partner. He found it in Paris and thought of me because I work part-time in an Italian restaurant and adore Italian food.

projects.title: Third Eye
projects.referencement: Foundation year project at ECAL, with Daniela Droz and Tonatiuh Ambrosetti.
projects.description:
For as long as I can remember, I have spent Christmas with my family—yet I remain a stranger among them. I know them only through the stories my parents tell and the faded images preserved in family archives. Beyond the bond of blood, there seems to be nothing that truly connects us.

I have chosen to immerse myself in these archives, tracing the fragments of their lives and memories in search of moments, gestures, or details that might bridge the distance between us.

projects.title: Shaped Nature
projects.referencement: First-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Catherine Leutenegger.
projects.description: A park is nature, but constructed. It is both a place of calm and of movement. A space where I walk, think, and notice. My photographs capture its dynamic side: spontaneous moments, traces of human presence, and the subtle structures that have merged with the landscape. Here, design and growth meet in contrast, tension, and harmony.

projects.title: Dans la Tête de Jules
projects.referencement: First-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Léonard Guyot, featuring Jules Monod.
projects.description: A video project delving into the inner world of artist Jules Monod. Using 3D scanning and modeling, I set out to recreate a dream he once described to me; a dream steeped in chaos and confusion. We wander through his art as though navigating the very architecture of his mind.

projects.title: Bob
projects.referencement: Proposed project for the ECAL Bachelor entrance competition.
projects.description: I chose to explore the theme of deepfakes, creating a universe around a generic character, who, in the end, does not exist. The project raises questions about identity and representation in the digital age.

projects.title: A guide to making good Italian coffee if you are a dog waiter
projects.referencement: First-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Guy Meldem, featuring Léo the Dog.
projects.description: Through this small illustrated booklet, I chose to feature our late dog as the main character, guiding us step by step on how to make a perfect cup of coffee. The booklet was created using a two-color process, with a green layer printed by laser and a pink layer overprinted in risograph.

projects.title: King, Typography
projects.referencement: First-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Robert Huber.
projects.description: Continuation of the hand-drawn lowercase project, creating a medium rounded weight.

projects.title: Play with the children of Prélaz
projects.referencement: First-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Adeline Mollard.
projects.description: We were tasked with creating a visual identity based on our neighborhood. In my case, Prélaz. I chose to work with the primary school located across from where I lived at the time. I collected and then deconstructed the playground games, with the goal of designing a new, modular game that could be adapted in countless ways. The pages of the edition can be detached, allowing the game to be rebuilt on the ground using its various components. Interspersed with sticker pages, the publication is designed to be playful and interactive.

projects.title: La Brocante
projects.referencement: Foundation year project at ECAL, with Violène Pont.
projects.description: A series of ten flyers promoting various second-hand events across the Canton of Vaud. I photographed vintage toys and handcrafted the paper, then printed each flyer at home using a small inkjet printer.

projects.title: 72 Flyers
projects.referencement: First-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Adeline Mollard.
projects.description: This catalog is a personal archive documenting my interest in graphic design from ages 20 to 24, coinciding with my entry into ÉCAL. It brings together 72 flyers, selected from over 200 collected between 2020 and 2024. My objective was to deconstruct the traditional flyer format to identify its core visual and material characteristics, such as size, color palette, typography, imagery, and composition. The collection is organized into three distinct categories: Standard flyers created by professional graphic designers. High-quality flyers, distinguished by superior paper, printing techniques, and design. Vernacular flyers, handmade by amateurs or non-designers, often with unique and spontaneous visual qualities. Through this selection, I explore how different approaches to design—professional, refined, or improvised—shape the visual identity and communicative power of a flyer

projects.title: The Greatest Show on Earth!(?), Freak Shows Through Headlines
projects.referencement: First-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Diego Bontognalli.
projects.description: The Greatest Show on Earth!(?), Freak Shows Through Headlines is an editorial project built from American newspaper archives (1865–2005) exploring how so-called “freak” artists, often marginalized and exhibited as curiosities, were represented in popular press for over a century. Through a collage of articles, photographs, dispatches, and fragmented stories, the project examines the thin line between entertainment and dehumanization. It contrasts the sensationalized, mocking, or fascinated public image shaped by the media with rare glimpses of personal lives—love, family, work. Newspapers are both source and material: by reframing and assembling them, the project offers a critical reading of these narratives, revealing moments of tenderness alongside systemic exploitation of bodies for spectacle. Ultimately, the work reflects on how media constructs stories from difference, raising questions that remain urgent today: Who is granted a full, nuanced, human narrative? Whose voices are amplified, and whose are silenced behind the spectacle? By recontextualizing these archives, the project uses graphic editing as a tool for critique, memory, and reclamation.

projects.title: Misery of the Hearts
projects.referencement: First-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Elodie Anglade.
projects.description: This project offers an animated reinterpretation of the visual work of Swiss artist Marianne Eigenheer, whose artistic language navigates between meditative stillness and expressive tension. Composed of three scenes designed and modeled in Cinema 4D, the work aims to translate the evocative power of her practice into a digital, moving medium. Presented as a triptych, these sequences extend the contemplative atmosphere and subtle melancholy of the original piece, while opening new visual possibilities through the interplay of light, texture, and rhythm.

projects.title: Menu for Casa Nostra
projects.referencement: Comission for the Casa Nostra restaurant in Martigny.
projects.description: After five years working in the Italian restaurant Casa Nostra, I have grown deeply attached to its warm family story and traditional atmosphere. To refresh the menu, I used hand-drawn illustrations and custom typography, blending tradition with a contemporary feel, blending with the décor and the restaurant’s elaborate pasta creations.

projects.title: "We are going to fight fire with marshmallows"
projects.referencement: First-year Bachelor workshop at ECAL, with Helas Studio.
projects.description: This poster is a reinterpretation of one of my favorite films, Toys by Barry Levinson, starring Robin Williams. My goal was to convey both the chaos and the sense of childhood innocence present in the story, while staying true to its plot without revealing any spoilers. Designed as a toy box, the poster’s interior scene was created in 3D using Blender.

projects.title: Femmes de Troie
projects.referencement: Comission for the Parhélia theatre troupe, Flavie Tapparel.
projects.description: Featuring Elisa Feraud’s art, I designed a poster for Femmes de Troie, a play by the Parhélia theatre troupe.

projects.title: FindFoot, We <3 Feet
projects.referencement: Second-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Anouk Schneider and Nicolas Polli. In collaboration with Colas Ravey.
projects.description: The graphic choices for this editorial project were inspired by our primary reference, FeetFinder. The color palette highlights its signature purple, while selected pages are printed on pink paper to feature the descriptions from our posts on the site. These bright inserts also nod to the “pink pages” of adult phone services. The small format and minimalist cover allow for discreet browsing, respecting the potential sensitivity of the content. The book’s structure is simple: it begins with a glossary of the site’s tags, each linked to one or more corresponding images, ensuring that even very specific interests are addressed. Overall, the project offers a playful yet thoughtful exploration of its subject matter.

projects.title: Digital Cleanup Week
projects.referencement: First-year Bachelor workshop at ECAL, with Angelo Benedetto and Vincent Jacquier. In collaboration with Amélie Mathey.
projects.description: Proposal for a series of three animated posters and one main poster for Digital Cleanup Week, organized by HES-SO. This collaborative project involved creating hand-drawn stop-motion animations, giving a playful and engaging character to the event’s visual identity.

projects.title: Arancino
projects.referencement: Second-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Aurèle Sack.
projects.description: Arancino is a fun and rounded typeface developed in two main stages. I began by sketching each letter by hand, experimenting with curves and shapes to give the font its playful character. After refining the sketches, I moved on to Glyphs, where I carefully drew both the uppercase and lowercase characters. The final result is a lively, approachable font that combines precision with a handcrafted feel, perfect for projects that want to convey friendliness, warmth, and personality.

projects.title: Cartoon Network used to rock but not anymore and that makes me sad
projects.referencement: Second-year Bachelor workshop at ECAL, with studio Balmer Hählen.
projects.description: This project consisted of creating a poster that captures the nostalgic feeling of a ‘Madeleine de Proust’ moment. I drew inspiration from my childhood memories of watching Cartoon Network, an experience I no longer have, which brings a bittersweet awareness of growing up. The poster was created using a two-color process: the first layer was printed with inkjet, and the second was screen-printed.

projects.title: Et si l'on pouvait comprendre les Oiseaux
projects.referencement: Second-year Bachelor workshop at ECAL, with Nicole Urdy.
projects.description: This project was created for the Maison de l’Île aux Oiseaux in Préverenges. It explores translating sensations into visual form, capturing sound, silence, and movement through images and blind embossing. A dialogue unfolds between person, bird, and the house itself, creating a cyclical rhythm. The book becomes a space of contemplation, where image, material, and memory intertwine to extend the original experience.

projects.title: CHEER Magazine
projects.referencement: Second-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Anouk Schneider and Emmanuel Crivelli. In collaboration with Colas Ravey.
projects.description: CHeer is a magazine that celebrates and highlights Swiss queer youth. Through personal stories, portraits, and cultural explorations, we give a voice to those who are shaping queer identities today. CHeer is a space for visibility, affirmation, and celebration, where experiences intersect and are shared. Because queer youth deserve to be heard, because stories are diverse, complex, and luminous, this magazine exists to document, inspire, and bring people together.

projects.title: Dys Typeface
projects.referencement: Third-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Aurèle Sack.
projects.description: Developed as an exploration of accessible typography, this typeface aims to facilitate reading for people with dyslexia. A deliberate concentration of visual weight in the lower part of each character reinforces orientation and helps differentiate similar letterforms. Several letters also feature alternative designs that break conventional symmetry, reducing potential confusion between mirrored or closely related characters. The project investigates how typographic form can actively support readability without compromising expressive design.

projects.title: Making Architecture Beautiful Again
projects.referencement: Third-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Jonathan Hares.
projects.description: The book examines a decree signed during Donald Trump’s presidency that proposed classical architecture as the mandatory style for new U.S. federal buildings. By sequencing the decree’s language alongside critical texts and analyses, the project explores how ideas of beauty, heritage, and national identity become political tools. Printed with gold silkscreen overlays, the book critiques the use of aesthetics as a vehicle for ideological control.

projects.title: Variety of Connecions
projects.referencement: Second-year Bachelor project at ECAL, with Adeline Mollard.
projects.description: Created as part of a visual identity course made with Gabriel Polgar, this project was selected as the official visual identity for the 2026 edition of the Antigel Festival, in Geneva. Inspired by the structure of a modular synthesizer, the identity translates the festival’s experimental spirit into a dynamic grid system based on the geographical locations of its venues. Animated, flexible, and constantly evolving, the visual language reflects Antigel’s ability to transform spaces, connect communities, and redefine cultural boundaries.
